Hernán Galíndez.bio: Argentine-born goalkeeper who became a naturalized Ecuadorian citizen in 2019 after years playing in Ecuador's Liga Pro. Made over 300 appearances for Universidad Católica before moving to Huracán, and represented Ecuador at the 2022 World Cup and two Copa América tournaments.
Hernán Galíndez.playingStyle: Complete goalkeeper with quick reflexes and confident distribution, comfortable playing out from the back
Hernán Galíndez.worldCup2026Role: Experienced backup goalkeeper providing tournament know-how and veteran leadership from the 2022 World Cup campaign
Hernán Galíndez.notableFact: Led Huracán to the 2025 Torneo Apertura final, saving two penalties in the semifinal shootout against Independiente

Félix Torres.bio: Physical center-back from San Lorenzo, Ecuador who has established himself as a regular in the national team's backline. Won the Paulista A1 and Copa with Corinthians in 2025 before joining Internacional on loan.
Félix Torres.playingStyle: Commanding aerial presence and aggressive tackler who excels in one-on-one defensive duels
Félix Torres.worldCup2026Role: Starting center-back partner forming Ecuador's experienced defensive spine alongside Hincapié or Pacho
Félix Torres.notableFact: Scored the match-winning goal against Uruguay in World Cup qualifying, assisted by teenage debutant Kendry Páez

Piero Hincapié.bio: Young left-footed center-back who has become a key starter at Bayer Leverkusen in the Bundesliga. At just 24, he is one of South America's most highly valued defenders and a cornerstone of Ecuador's backline.
Piero Hincapié.playingStyle: Ball-playing center-back with excellent passing range, composed under pressure with the ability to drive forward from defense
Piero Hincapié.worldCup2026Role: Starting center-back and primary ball-progressor from the defensive line, instrumental in Ecuador's build-up play
Piero Hincapié.notableFact: Was part of Bayer Leverkusen's historic 2023-24 unbeaten Bundesliga title-winning season

Robert Arboleda.bio: Veteran center-back who has spent the majority of his career in Brazilian football with São Paulo FC. A long-standing member of the national team with over 45 caps, providing experience and stability at the back.
Robert Arboleda.playingStyle: Traditional no-nonsense center-back who prioritizes defensive solidity and commanding aerial presence
Robert Arboleda.worldCup2026Role: Veteran defensive option and squad depth player who can step in when physicality and experience are needed
Robert Arboleda.notableFact: Has played over a decade in Brazilian Serie A, one of the most competitive leagues in South America

José Cifuentes.bio: Energetic central midfielder who has played across the Americas, including stints in MLS with LAFC before moving to Brazilian football with Cruzeiro. Known for his box-to-box engine and tenacity.
José Cifuentes.playingStyle: Dynamic box-to-box midfielder with high work rate and the ability to cover large areas of the pitch
José Cifuentes.worldCup2026Role: Midfield rotation option providing energy and defensive cover, capable of featuring as a substitute to change the tempo
José Cifuentes.notableFact: Won the MLS Supporters' Shield with LAFC and scored crucial goals during their championship run

Byron Castillo.bio: Attacking right-back from Barcelona SC in Ecuador's Liga Pro who became the center of an eligibility controversy involving Chile ahead of the 2022 World Cup. FIFA ultimately ruled in Ecuador's favor, allowing the team to compete.
Byron Castillo.playingStyle: Overlapping right-back with pace and crossing ability, more attacking than defensive in his approach
Byron Castillo.worldCup2026Role: Backup right-back option providing width and attacking impetus from the bench
Byron Castillo.notableFact: Was at the center of a major FIFA eligibility dispute in 2022 when Chile challenged his nationality, but Ecuador's World Cup qualification was upheld

Pervis Estupiñán.bio: Dynamic left-back who became the first Ecuadorian player in AC Milan's history after signing from Brighton in July 2025. Previously starred in the Premier League with Brighton and represented Ecuador at the 2022 World Cup.
Pervis Estupiñán.playingStyle: Modern attacking full-back with explosive pace, tireless overlapping runs, and quality final-ball delivery
Pervis Estupiñán.worldCup2026Role: Undisputed starting left-back and Ecuador's primary threat down the left flank, providing width and creativity in the final third
Pervis Estupiñán.notableFact: Became AC Milan's first-ever Ecuadorian player when he signed for the club in July 2025

Carlos Gruezo.bio: Experienced defensive midfielder who has played extensively in European and North American football, including spells in the Bundesliga with Stuttgart and Augsburg before settling in Mexico's Liga MX with Santos Laguna.
Carlos Gruezo.playingStyle: Disciplined defensive midfielder who screens the back four, wins possession, and keeps things simple in distribution
Carlos Gruezo.worldCup2026Role: Experienced midfield anchor providing defensive stability and ball-winning presence in deeper positions
Carlos Gruezo.notableFact: Has accumulated over 50 caps for Ecuador, making him one of the most experienced players in the current squad

Enner Valencia.bio: Ecuador's all-time leading scorer and iconic captain with over 40 international goals. Has scored at three World Cups (2014, 2022) and holds the record for most World Cup goals by an Ecuadorian player with six consecutive tournament goals.
Enner Valencia.playingStyle: Complete forward with aerial prowess, intelligent movement, and clinical finishing in big moments
Enner Valencia.worldCup2026Role: Captain and talisman leading the attack, likely as a starter or super-sub providing leadership and World Cup experience
Enner Valencia.notableFact: Scored six consecutive World Cup goals across 2014 and 2022 tournaments, including a brace in the opening match of Qatar 2022 against the hosts

Moisés Caicedo.bio: Ecuador's midfield maestro and Chelsea star who transferred for a British-record fee of around 115 million pounds in 2023. Widely regarded as one of the best defensive midfielders in the world alongside Manchester City's Rodri.
Moisés Caicedo.playingStyle: Elite ball-winning midfielder who combines relentless pressing with excellent passing range and the ability to drive forward with the ball
Moisés Caicedo.worldCup2026Role: The heartbeat of Ecuador's midfield and most important player — controls the tempo, breaks up play, and links defense to attack
Moisés Caicedo.notableFact: Chelsea manager Enzo Maresca stated that Caicedo and Rodri are the two best defensive midfielders in the world

Jeremy Sarmiento.bio: Spanish-born winger of Ecuadorian descent who came through the youth ranks at Charlton Athletic and Benfica before joining Brighton. Has had productive loan spells in the Championship, helping Ipswich win promotion to the Premier League.
Jeremy Sarmiento.playingStyle: Tricky inverted winger with quick feet, dribbling ability, and the pace to beat defenders one-on-one
Jeremy Sarmiento.worldCup2026Role: Attacking option off the bench or as a rotational winger bringing pace and directness to unsettle tired defenses
Jeremy Sarmiento.notableFact: Born in Madrid and raised in London, he chose to represent Ecuador at senior level and was part of the 2022 World Cup squad alongside Brighton teammates Estupiñán and Caicedo

Alexander Domínguez.bio: Veteran goalkeeper and one of Ecuador's most experienced players with over 60 international caps. A stalwart of Liga de Quito and a longtime fixture in the national team setup across multiple qualifying cycles.
Alexander Domínguez.playingStyle: Experienced shot-stopper with strong command of the penalty area and vocal leadership of the defensive line
Alexander Domínguez.worldCup2026Role: Third-choice goalkeeper providing veteran experience and dressing room leadership for the squad
Alexander Domínguez.notableFact: Has represented Ecuador at both Copa América and World Cup qualifying across more than a decade of international service

Angelo Preciado.bio: Right-back who has been playing European football with Sparta Prague in the Czech First League after making a name for himself in Ecuador's domestic football. A regular in the national team with over 35 caps.
Angelo Preciado.playingStyle: Energetic and physically strong right-back who combines defensive solidity with forward runs and crossing ability
Angelo Preciado.worldCup2026Role: Starting right-back contender who provides balance between attacking thrust and defensive discipline on the right flank
Angelo Preciado.notableFact: Was part of Ecuador's 2022 World Cup squad and has gained valuable European experience playing in continental competitions with Sparta Prague

Xavier Arreaga.bio: Left-footed center-back who has built his career in MLS with the Seattle Sounders after starting in Ecuadorian domestic football. Brings familiarity with playing in the United States, which could be valuable at a home-continent World Cup.
Xavier Arreaga.playingStyle: Left-footed center-back comfortable in possession with steady defensive positioning and an ability to step into midfield with the ball
Xavier Arreaga.worldCup2026Role: Defensive depth option who brings left-footed balance and experience playing in US stadiums that will host World Cup matches
Xavier Arreaga.notableFact: Has been playing in MLS since 2019 with the Seattle Sounders, giving him extensive experience in American football venues

Joao Rojas.bio: Veteran winger with over a decade of professional experience in Ecuadorian and international football. A quick, tricky attacker who has represented Ecuador on the international stage for years, known for his pace along the flanks.
Joao Rojas.playingStyle: Old-school winger with blistering pace, direct dribbling, and an ability to stretch defenses with his speed
Joao Rojas.worldCup2026Role: Experienced squad player who can provide a late-game spark with his speed off the bench
Joao Rojas.notableFact: At 37, he would be one of the oldest players in the Ecuadorian squad, bringing invaluable experience from over 30 international caps

Kendry Páez.bio: Generational Ecuadorian prodigy who signed for Chelsea at age 16 and is currently on loan at River Plate in Argentina. Became the youngest player to represent Ecuador's senior team and the youngest South American to score in a World Cup qualifier.
Kendry Páez.playingStyle: Creative attacking midfielder with exceptional vision, technical ability beyond his years, and a dangerous left foot for through balls and set pieces
Kendry Páez.worldCup2026Role: Creative spark and potential game-changer off the bench or as a starter, providing the unpredictability and invention Ecuador need to break down organized defenses
Kendry Páez.notableFact: At just 19 years old during the World Cup, he will be one of the youngest players in the tournament, having already debuted internationally at 16 behind only Diego Maradona as the youngest South American to play senior international football

Nilson Angulo.bio: Young left-back who plays for LAFC in MLS, bringing pace and energy down the left side. Part of Ecuador's emerging generation of fullbacks and has represented the national team at youth and senior levels.
Nilson Angulo.playingStyle: Pacey attacking left-back who loves to get forward and deliver crosses, providing width and dynamism on the left flank
Nilson Angulo.worldCup2026Role: Backup left-back providing depth behind Estupiñán and an attacking option to change the dynamic of the flank
Nilson Angulo.notableFact: Plays alongside fellow Ecuadorian Diego Palacios at LAFC in MLS, bringing familiarity with US football environments to the World Cup

Alan Franco.bio: Versatile center-back who plays for Atlético Mineiro in Brazil's Serie A. Has shown the ability to play in both central defense and defensive midfield, adding tactical flexibility to Ecuador's squad.
Alan Franco.playingStyle: Composed and technically proficient defender who can play out from the back and occasionally step into midfield roles
Alan Franco.worldCup2026Role: Versatile defensive option capable of playing center-back or defensive midfielder, providing tactical flexibility from the bench
Alan Franco.notableFact: Has experience in Brazil's highly competitive Serie A with Atlético Mineiro, developing his game in one of South America's toughest leagues

Gonzalo Plata.bio: Explosive right winger who joined Flamengo in 2024 and scored the decisive goal in the Copa do Brasil final against Atlético Mineiro. A left-footed inverted winger with pace and flair who has been a regular for Ecuador since his teenage years.
Gonzalo Plata.playingStyle: Explosive inverted winger who cuts inside from the right flank onto his stronger left foot, combining blistering pace with skillful dribbling
Gonzalo Plata.worldCup2026Role: Starting right winger providing the creative spark and individual brilliance needed to unlock stubborn defenses with his pace and trickery
Gonzalo Plata.notableFact: Scored the winning goal in the 2024 Copa do Brasil final for Flamengo, one of the biggest domestic cup finals in South American football

Jordy Caicedo.bio: Target-man striker playing in Argentina's Liga Profesional with Barracas Central. A tall, physical forward who offers a different profile to the other attackers in Ecuador's squad with his aerial presence.
Jordy Caicedo.playingStyle: Traditional target forward who excels in aerial duels, hold-up play, and creating space for teammates with his physical presence
Jordy Caicedo.worldCup2026Role: Alternative striking option off the bench when Ecuador needs to change their attacking approach and go more direct with long balls and crosses
Jordy Caicedo.notableFact: At 189cm tall, he is one of the tallest forwards in Ecuador's squad, providing a Plan B option in attack

Alan Minda.bio: Young right winger developing in Belgian football with Lommel SK, part of the City Football Group network. A pacy and direct attacker who represents Ecuador's exciting young generation of wingers.
Alan Minda.playingStyle: Direct and pacy winger who takes on defenders with speed and trickery, looking to beat his man and deliver crosses or cut inside
Alan Minda.worldCup2026Role: Young attacking option off the bench providing fresh legs and directness in the final third during later stages of matches
Alan Minda.notableFact: Plays for Lommel SK, which is part of the City Football Group that also owns Manchester City, developing within one of football's most prominent multi-club networks

Moisés Ramírez.bio: Young goalkeeper from Independiente del Valle, the renowned Ecuadorian academy that has produced talents like Moisés Caicedo and Kendry Páez. Represents the future of Ecuador's goalkeeping department.
Moisés Ramírez.playingStyle: Modern goalkeeper comfortable with the ball at his feet, trained in Independiente del Valle's possession-oriented philosophy
Moisés Ramírez.worldCup2026Role: Young third goalkeeper gaining World Cup squad experience and developing for future tournaments alongside veteran keepers
Moisés Ramírez.notableFact: Product of Independiente del Valle's celebrated academy, the same institution that developed Moisés Caicedo and Kendry Páez

Willian Pacho.bio: Elite center-back who starred at Paris Saint-Germain, starting in the 2025 Champions League final victory over Inter Milan to become the first Ecuadorian to win the competition. Signed for PSG in a deal worth up to 45 million euros.
Willian Pacho.playingStyle: Left-footed ball-playing center-back with excellent passing range, strong in interceptions and tackles, capable of building play from the back
Willian Pacho.worldCup2026Role: Starting center-back and key figure in Ecuador's defensive structure, combining with Hincapié to form one of the most talented young center-back pairings in the tournament
Willian Pacho.notableFact: Became the first Ecuadorian player to win the UEFA Champions League when PSG beat Inter Milan 5-0 in the 2025 final

Diego Palacios.bio: Left-back who plays for LAFC in MLS alongside fellow Ecuadorian Nilson Angulo. A technically gifted defender who offers quality going forward and has represented Ecuador regularly at international level.
Diego Palacios.playingStyle: Technical left-back with a good passing range and the ability to combine with wingers in the final third
keyStrengths: Understanding of MLS and US environments
Diego Palacios.worldCup2026Role: Defensive depth option at left-back who brings familiarity with US stadiums and environments for the North American World Cup
Diego Palacios.notableFact: Has been based in the United States with LAFC since 2020, making him one of the most experienced Ecuador players in terms of living and playing in World Cup host country USA

John Yeboah.bio: Left winger of Ghanaian-Ecuadorian heritage playing in Belgian football. A quick and direct attacker who adds further depth to Ecuador's already well-stocked pool of wingers.
John Yeboah.playingStyle: Pacy winger who uses his speed to beat defenders on the outside and deliver crosses into the box
John Yeboah.worldCup2026Role: Squad depth option on the wing providing an additional attacking alternative from the bench
John Yeboah.notableFact: Has a dual Ghanaian-Ecuadorian background, choosing to represent Ecuador at international level and bringing a unique multicultural perspective to the squad

Kevin Rodríguez.bio: Young striker playing for Metz in France's Ligue 2, gaining valuable European experience. A versatile forward capable of playing across the front line who is developing rapidly in the French football system.
Kevin Rodríguez.playingStyle: Mobile and hardworking forward who presses from the front, links play, and can finish with both feet
Kevin Rodríguez.worldCup2026Role: Young forward depth option capable of providing energy and pressing from the bench in the latter stages of matches
Kevin Rodríguez.notableFact: At 25, he represents the middle ground of Ecuador's squad between the veteran attackers and the teenage sensation Kendry Páez
